perturbation.original_text: You will be responsible for any breach of this agreement by you or your representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: You will be responsible for any breach of this agreement by you, except for breaches resulting from circumstances beyond your direct control or negligence, such as force majeure events.
perturbation.explanation: Originally, the text held the party fully responsible for any breach of the agreement by them or their representatives, without exception. This modification introduces an exception for breaches caused by events outside of their control like 'force majeure events'. This contradicts the original blanket responsibility, making it unclear when responsibility applies. Adding exemptions affects other clauses of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You will be responsible for any breach of this agreement by you or your representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the party is responsible for any breach of the agreement without exceptions. The changed text introduces an exception for breaches caused by events outside of their control. This directly contradicts the blanket responsibility stated in the contradicted text, which also states that the party is responsible for any breach of the agreement without exceptions, creating a clear inconsistency and uncertainty regarding liability. This aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: Securities Law Compliance You acknowledge that the Evaluation Materials represent, in part, material, non-public information regarding the Company and its prospects. Accordingly, you acknowledge that you and your representatives understand that the securities laws of the United States prohibit any person who has material, non-public information concerning the Company or a Possible Transaction from purchasing or selling securities in reliance upon such information or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities in reliance upon such information.
perturbation.changed_text: Securities Law Compliance You acknowledge that while some of the Evaluation Materials may contain non-public information, the Company does not guarantee its materiality and does not advise on actions regarding securities. You and your representatives are solely responsible for compliance with securities laws.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly acknowledges that the Evaluation Materials include material, non-public information, and reminds the recipient of their obligation to comply with securities laws. The modified text diminishes this clarity by stating that the Company does not guarantee the materiality of the information. This contradiction creates uncertainty. The change contradicts the initial acknowledgment that the materials contain crucial information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Securities Law Compliance You acknowledge that the Evaluation Materials represent, in part, material, non-public information regarding the Company and its prospects. Accordingly, you acknowledge that you and your representatives understand that the securities laws of the United States prohibit any person who has material, non-public information concerning the Company or a Possible Transaction from purchasing or selling securities in reliance upon such information or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities in reliance upon such information.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Evaluation Materials contain material, non-public information, while the changed text weakens this by stating the Company does not guarantee its materiality. This introduces a contradiction because the contradicted text affirms the presence of material information, while the changed text implies the information's materiality is uncertain, creating a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the Company’s request, you shall promptly deliver to the Company or destroy all written Evaluation Materials and any other written materials without retaining, in whole or in part, any copies, extracts or other reproductions (whatever the form or storage medium) of such materials, and shall certify the destruction of such materials in writing to the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the Company’s request, you shall make a good faith effort to return or destroy all Evaluation Materials; however, you may retain copies necessary for internal compliance purposes, provided you maintain their confidentiality.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates complete return or destruction of all Evaluation Materials without retaining copies. The modified text introduces an exception allowing retention of copies for 'internal compliance purposes'. This directly contradicts the original requirement of no copies being retained. The 'internal compliance' exception is vague, creating uncertainty about what qualifies and under what conditions it is allowed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon the Company’s request, you shall promptly deliver to the Company or destroy all written Evaluation Materials and any other written materials without retaining, in whole or in part, any copies, extracts or other reproductions (whatever the form or storage medium) of such materials, and shall certify the destruction of such materials in writing to the Company.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates complete return or destruction of Evaluation Materials without retaining copies, while the changed text introduces an exception allowing retention of copies for 'internal compliance purposes'. The contradicted text repeats the original text, mandating no copies. This directly contradicts the original requirement of no copies being retained, therefore it is a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: You understand and agree that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this agreement by you or your representatives and that the Company will be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief as remedies for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this agreement by you or your representatives but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity.
perturbation.changed_text: While the Company may seek specific performance or injunctive relief, it acknowledges that monetary damages may, in some circumstances, provide an adequate remedy. The Company reserves the right to pursue any available remedy, but the choice of remedy will be at its sole discretion, and will not be interpreted to mean that monetary damages is not a sufficient remedy.
perturbation.explanation: Originally, the text explicitly states that money damages would *not* be a sufficient remedy for breaches of the agreement, entitling the company to specific performance/injunctive relief. The modified version introduces a contradiction by stating that monetary damages *may* be adequate in some circumstances. This creates uncertainty about what remedy the company can seek. The modification casts doubt on the sufficiency of monetary damages.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You understand and agree that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this agreement by you or your representatives and that the Company will be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief as remedies for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this agreement by you or your representatives but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity.
perturbation.justification: The original text and contradicted text state that money damages are *not* a sufficient remedy. The changed text says monetary damages *may* be adequate in some circumstances. This is a direct contradiction of the original statement, creating uncertainty about the enforceability of specific performance or injunctive relief. This aligns with the definition of a structural flaw causing an in-text contradiction.
